hypotheses.name: SV2-4 repulsion mechanistic probe: TF class separation
hypotheses.method: At L0: SVD of centered nonzero embeddings [2039,512]. Project to SV2-4 (Vt[1:4]) and SV5-7 (Vt[4:7]). Extract 78 TF positions in nz space. Sample 500 TF-TF pairs, compare cosine AUROC vs random pairs. Also compare to TF-target edge AUROC (n=589). TF-TF AUROC > 0.50 => TFs cluster in that subspace; TF-target AUROC < 0.50 => edges are anti-clustered.
hypotheses.primary_metric: TF-TF AUROC vs TF-target AUROC in SV2-4
hypotheses.result_value: TF-TF SV2-4=0.5387, TF-target SV2-4=0.4849, TF-TF SV5-7=0.5284, TF-target SV5-7=0.5989
hypotheses.next_action: Replicate across layers; test non-TF/random-TF pairs as additional control

hypotheses.name: Signed regulation AUROC split: Activation vs Repression in SV5-7 and SV2-4
hypotheses.method: Match cycle4_immune positive edges to TRRUST regulation type (Activation n=270, Repression n=141 after nonzero filter). Compute cosine-similarity AUROC in SV5-7 and SV2-4 for each group vs random gene pairs. Compare AUROC between regulation types.
hypotheses.primary_metric: Repression vs Activation AUROC difference in SV5-7
hypotheses.result_value: Repression SV5-7=0.6202, Activation SV5-7=0.5989 (delta=+0.021); Repression SV2-4=0.5497, Activation SV2-4=0.4657 (delta=+0.084)
hypotheses.next_action: Permutation test on regulation-label shuffle to confirm the activation/repression AUROC gap is not by chance; cross-seed replication

hypotheses.name: Systematic subspace scan SV(k,k+2) for k=0..9 at L0 with Bonferroni
hypotheses.method: For k=0..9: project centered nonzero embeddings [2039,512] to SV(k, k+1, k+2) using rows k:k+3 of Vt. Compute cosine-similarity AUROC for 589 TF-target positive edges vs random pairs. Permutation test N=100. Bonferroni correction (10 windows).
hypotheses.result_value: Best raw AUROC: SV8-10=0.619 (perm_p=0.67); SV5-7=0.611 (perm_p=0.28); no window survives Bonferroni (all perm_p_bonf>=0.2)
hypotheses.next_action: Redo with N=500 permutations to gain power for Bonferroni correction (minimum detectable p=0.002)
